div.h1: Cache Misses: Chained vs Open Addressing

p.strong: Performance Analysis of Hash Table Memory Access Patterns
p.em: Open addressing shows 2.5x to 4.0x reduction across all cache hierarchy levels.

div.p: Metric: Millions of cache misses Workload: 10M lookups / 1M entries Hardware: x86_64, 64B Cache Line

g.title: Chained: High pointer chasing overhead. Each bucket is a linked list.
Open: Sequential array access fits cache lines perfectly.
Scattered heap allocations for nodes bypass L2 prefetchers.
Contiguous layout allows L2 hardware prefetch to predict next probes.
Large working set + pointers = LLC eviction pressure.
Dense bit-packed entries keep working set within LLC.

ul.li: Each pointer dereference is a dependent load. The CPU stalls waiting for RAM.
Open Addressing: Modern CPUs fetch 64 bytes at a time.
A single DRAM access brings 4 entries into L1 simultaneously.
Hardware Prefetchers proactively fetch subsequent lines.

blockquote.p: Result: Dramatically reduced CPU Pipeline Stalls.

g.title: Chained: High pointer chasing overhead. Each bucket is a linked list.
Open: Sequential array access fits cache lines perfectly.
Scattered heap allocations for nodes bypass L2 prefetchers.
Contiguous layout allows L2 hardware prefetch to predict next probes.
Large working set + pointers = LLC eviction pressure.
Dense bit-packed entries keep working set within LLC.
